item.title: About me
item.description:
Hello I am Java, I am a high school student who likes to make things. I have a love for design and prototyping, especially when it comes to turning ideas into real, working systems.

I enjoy programming and building projects in areas like robotics, electronics, and mechatronics. I also spend a lot of time working on 3D printing and mechanical design, including mechanisms and linkages, and I like exploring how physical systems move and interact.

item.title: Aquaponics Sensor System
item.description:
Java Murray.

Project Goal

The goal of this project was to design and build a low cost aquaponics monitoring system capable of collecting and displaying real-time environmental data. The system needed to be reliable, easy to maintain, and simple for users to monitor and calibrate.

Design Philosophy

My design philosophy is to solve problems using the simplest and most effective solution possible. I believe that systems should be reliable, easy to understand, and cost effective. Throughout this project, I focused on simplifying both the hardware and software while still providing accurate monitoring and a good user experience.

item.title: Contact Information
item.description:
📬 Contact Me

Hi there! Feel free to reach out if you have questions, want to collaborate, or just want to say hello.

item.title: Designing an Ornithopter
item.description:
Java Murray

DMA Project

Project Overview

This year, I’ve been working on a series of small, focused projects that all contribute to a larger goal, of designing and building an ornithopter. Each task helped me learn new skills in 3D design, prototyping, and engineering, all leading to my final concept.

item.title: Hydrontank Design
item.description:
Project Overview

This project started when Dr. Winter from Quantum Technology approached me with a design challenge: how would you suspend a hydrogen tank inside a vacuum chamber?

I was then asked to develop a CAD model of the concept we discussed so it could be included in a project proposal.

The Idea

The concept was to use Kevlar cables to suspend the tank inside the chamber. This would allow the position of the tank to be adjusted by tightening or loosening the cables, enabling fine alignment and controlled positioning within the system.

item.title: Java Murray
item.description:
Contact: java@javamuuray.com

Experience

  • Sailing Coach: I coach sailing camps during the summer, working with youth aged 7–16. I teach and mentor sailors through the CANSail Levels 1–4 program, helping them develop sailing skills, confidence, and teamwork on the water.

Volunteer

  • First Robotics Team Role Mechatronics: I help in CAD, Software, Electronics. Designing and prototyping Our Teams robot. Facing real world engineering, logistical, and management challenges.

  • Sound Lead Technician: I was the sound lead for the Argyle Drama Theatre Production program, where I managed a team of four people. Where I learn how to manage and lead a team whille also being a team player.
